translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after the death of Moses the servant of Jehovah, that Jehovah spoke to Joshua the son of Nun, Moses' attendant, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moses my servant is dead; and now, rise up, go over this Jordan, thou and all this people, into the land which I give unto them, to the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every place whereon the sole of your foot shall tread have I given to you, as I said unto Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From the wilderness and this Lebanon to the great river, the river Euphrates, the whole land of the Hittites, to the great sea, toward the going down of the sun, shall be your border.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: None shall be able to stand before thee all the days of thy life: as I was with Moses, so will I be with thee; I will not leave thee, neither will I forsake thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be strong and courageous, for thou shalt cause this people to inherit the land which I have sworn unto their fathers to give them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only be strong and very courageous, that thou mayest take heed to do according to all the law that Moses my servant commanded thee. Turn not from it to the right or to the left, that thou mayest prosper whithersoever thou goest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This book of the law shall not depart from thy mouth; and thou shalt meditate upon it day and night, that thou mayest take heed to do according to all that is written therein; for then shalt thou have good success in thy ways, and then shalt thou prosper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have I not commanded thee: Be strong and courageous? Be not afraid, neither be dismayed; for Jehovah thyGod is with thee whithersoever thou goest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua commanded the officers of the people, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go through the midst of the camp, and command the people, saying, Prepare yourselves victuals, for in three days ye shall pass over this Jordan, that ye may enter in to take possession of the land which Jehovah yourGod giveth you to possess it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And to the Reubenites and to the Gadites and to half the tribe of Manasseh spoke Joshua, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember the word that Moses the servant of Jehovah commanded you, saying, Jehovah yourGod has given you rest and has given you this land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Your wives, your little ones, and your cattle shall abide in the land that Moses gave you on this side of the Jordan, but ye, all the valiant men, shall go over in array before your brethren and help them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: until Jehovah give your brethren rest as to you, and they also take possession of the land which Jehovah yourGod giveth them; then shall ye return into the land of your possession and possess it, which Moses the servant of Jehovah gave you on this side of the Jordan toward the sun-rising.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they answered Joshua, saying, All that thou hast commanded us will we do, and whither thou shalt send us will we go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: According as we hearkened to Moses in all things, so will we hearken to thee; only may Jehovah thyGod be with thee as he was with Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every one that is rebellious against thy commandment and hearkeneth not to thy words in everything that thou commandest us, shall be put to death. Only be strong and courageous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua the son of Nun sent from Shittim two spies secretly, saying, Go, see the land, even Jericho. And they went, and came into a harlot's house, named Rahab, and they lay down there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told the king of Jericho, saying, Behold, men have come hither to-night from the children of Israel to search out the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Jericho sent to Rahab, saying, Bring forth the men that have come to thee, who have come into thy house: for they have come to search out all the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the woman had taken and concealed the two men; and she said, Yes, the men did come unto me, but I knew not whence they were;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and it came to pass when the gate had to be closed, at dark, that the men went out: I know not whither the men have gone. Pursue after them quickly; for ye shall overtake them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But she had taken them up to the roof, and secreted them under the stalks of flax, which she had laid out on the roof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men pursued after them the way to the Jordan, to the fords; and when they who pursued after them had gone out, they closed the gate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And before they had lain down, she went up to them upon the roof;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to the men, I know that Jehovah has given you the land, and that the dread of you has fallen on us, and that all the inhabitants of the land faint because of you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we have heard that Jehovah dried up the waters of the Red sea before you when ye came out of Egypt; and what ye did to the two kings of the Amorites that were beyond the Jordan, to Sihon and to Og, whom ye utterly destroyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We heard [of it], and our heart melted, and there remained no more spirit in any man because of you; for Jehovah yourGod, he isGod in the heavens above and on the earth beneath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, I pray you, swear to me by Jehovah, since I have dealt kindly with you, that ye will also deal kindly with my father's house, and give me a certain sign,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that ye will let my father live, and my mother, and my brethren, and my sisters, and all that belong to them, and deliver our souls from death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men said to her, Our lives shall pay for yours, if ye do not make this our business known; and it shall be when Jehovah shall give us the land, that we will deal kindly and truly with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she let them down by a cord through the window; for her house was upon the city-wall, and she dwelt upon the wall.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to them, Go to the mountain, that the pursuers may not meet with you; and hide yourselves there three days, until the pursuers have returned; and afterwards go your way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men said to her, We will be quit of this thine oath which thou hast made us swear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, when we come into the land, thou shalt bind in the window this line of scarlet thread by which thou hast let us down; and thou shalt gather to thee in the house thy father, and thy mother, and thy brethren, and all thy father's household;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and it shall be, that whoever shall go out of the doors of thy house into the street, — his blood shall be upon his head, and we shall be innocent; but every one who shall be with thee in the house, his blood shall be upon our head, if any hand be upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thou make known this our business, we will be quit of thine oath which thou hast made us swear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said, According to your words, so be it. And she sent them away, and they departed. And she bound the scarlet line in the window.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went, and came to the mountain, and remained there three days, until the pursuers had returned; and the pursuers sought them all the way, and found them not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the two men returned and came down from the mountain, and went over and came to Joshua the son of Nun, and related to him everything that had happened to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to Joshua, Of a surety Jehovah has given the whole land into our hands, and even all the inhabitants of the land faint because of us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua rose early in the morning; and they removed from Shittim, and came to the Jordan, he and all the children of Israel, and lodged there before they passed over.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the end of three days, that the officers went through the camp;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they commanded the people, saying, When ye see the ark of the covenant of Jehovah yourGod, and the priests the Levites bearing it, then remove from your place, and go after it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: yet there shall be a distance between you and it, about two thousand cubits by measure. Ye shall not come near it, that ye may know the way by which ye must go; for ye have not passed this way heretofore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua said to the people, Hallow yourselves; for to-morrow Jehovah will do wonders in your midst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua spoke to the priests, saying, Take up the ark of the covenant, and go over before the people. And they took up the ark of the covenant, and went before the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Joshua, This day will I begin to magnify thee in the sight of all Israel, that they may know that as I was with Moses, so will I be with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt command the priests who bear the ark of the covenant, saying, When ye come to the edge of the waters of the Jordan, stand still in the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua said to the children of Israel, Come hither, and hear the words of Jehovah yourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua said, Hereby shall ye know that the livingGod is in your midst, and [that] he will without fail dispossess from before you the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Hivites, and the Perizzites, and the Girgashites, and the Amorites, and the Jebusites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, the ark of the covenant of the Lord of all the earth is going over before you into the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now take you twelve men out of the tribes of Israel, one man for each tribe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when the soles of the feet of the priests who bear the ark of Jehovah, the Lord of all the earth, rest in the waters of the Jordan, the waters of the Jordan, the waters flowing down from above, shall be cut off, and shall stand up in a heap.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the people removed from their tents, to pass over the Jordan, that the priests bearing the ark of the covenant were before the people;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and when they that bore the ark were come to the Jordan, and the feet of the priests who bore the ark dipped in the edge of the water (and the Jordan is full over all its banks throughout the days of harvest),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the waters which flowed down from above stood [and] rose up in a heap, very far, by Adam, the city that is beside Zaretan; and those that flowed down towards the sea of the plain, the salt sea, were completely cut off. And the people went over opposite to Jericho.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priests who bore the ark of the covenant of Jehovah stood firm on dry ground in the midst of the Jordan. And all Israel went over on dry ground, until all the nation had completely gone over the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the whole nation had completely gone over the Jordan, that Jehovah spoke to Joshua, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take you twelve men out of the people, one man out of every tribe,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and command them, saying, Take up hence out of the midst of the Jordan, from the place where the priests' feet stood firm, twelve stones, and carry them over with you, and lay them down in the lodging-place where ye shall lodge this night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua called the twelve men, whom he had appointed of the children of Israel, a man out of every tribe;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Joshua said to them, Pass before the ark of Jehovah yourGod into the midst of the Jordan, and lift up each of you a stone [and put it] upon his shoulder, according to the number of the tribes of the children of Israel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that this may be a sign in your midst. When your children ask hereafter, saying, What mean ye by these stones?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then ye shall say to them, That the waters of the Jordan were cut off before the ark of the covenant of Jehovah; when it went through the Jordan, the waters of the Jordan were cut off. And these stones shall be for a memorial unto the children of Israel for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel did so, as Joshua had commanded, and took up twelve stones out of the midst of the Jordan, as Jehovah had spoken to Joshua, according to the number of the tribes of the children of Israel; and they carried them over with them to the lodging-place, and laid them down there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And twelve stones did Joshua set up in the midst of the Jordan, in the place where the feet of the priests who bore the ark of the covenant had stood firm; and they are there to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priests who bore the ark stood in the midst of the Jordan, until everything was finished that Jehovah had commanded Joshua to speak unto the people, according to all that Moses had commanded Joshua. And the people hasted and passed over.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when all the people had completely gone over, that the ark of Jehovah went over, and the priests, in the presence of the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Reuben, and the children of Gad, and the half tribe of Manasseh, went over in array before the children of Israel, as Moses had spoken to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: About forty thousand armed for military service passed over before Jehovah to the war, unto the plains of Jericho.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On that day Jehovah magnified Joshua in the sight of all Israel; and they feared him, as they had feared Moses, all the days of his life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Joshua, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Command the priests who bear the ark of the testimony, that they come up out of the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua commanded the priests, saying, Come up out of the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the priests who bore the ark of the covenant of Jehovah had come up out of the midst of the Jordan, [when] the soles of the priests' feet were lifted up on to the dry land, that the waters of the Jordan returned to their place, and they flowed as previously, over all its banks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people came up out of the Jordan on the tenth of the first month, and encamped in Gilgal, on the eastern extremity of Jericho.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those twelve stones which they had taken out of the Jordan did Joshua set up in Gilgal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke to the children of Israel, saying, When your children hereafter ask their fathers, saying, What [mean] these stones?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then ye shall let your children know, saying, On dry land did Israel come over this Jordan;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because Jehovah yourGod dried up the waters of the Jordan from before you, until ye had passed over, as Jehovah yourGod did to the Red sea, which he dried up from before us, until we had passed over;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that all peoples of the earth might know the hand of Jehovah, that it is mighty; that ye might fear Jehovah yourGod continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when all the kings of the Amorites, who were beyond the Jordan westward, and all the kings of the Canaanites, who were by the sea, heard that Jehovah had dried up the waters of the Jordan from before the children of Israel, until they had passed over, that their heart melted, and there was no spirit in them any more, because of the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time Jehovah said to Joshua, Make thee stone-knives, and circumcise again the children of Israel the second time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua made him stone-knives, and circumcised the children of Israel at the hill of Araloth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the cause why Joshua circumcised [them]: all the people that had come out of Egypt, the males, all the men of war, had died in the wilderness on the way, after they came out of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For all the people that came out were circumcised; but all the people that were born in the wilderness on the way, after they came out of Egypt, [them] had they not circumcised.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the children of Israel had walked forty years in the wilderness, till the whole nation of men of war had perished who had come out of Egypt, who had not hearkened to the voice of Jehovah; to whom Jehovah had sworn that he would not show them the land which Jehovah had sworn unto their fathers that he would give us, a land flowing with milk and honey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their sons [whom] he raised up in their stead, them Joshua circumcised, for they were uncircumcised, because they had not circumcised them on the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the whole nation had finished being circumcised, that they abode in their place in the camp, till they were whole.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Joshua, This day have I rolled away the reproach of Egypt from off you. And the name of the place was called Gilgal to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel encamped in Gilgal, and held the passover on the fourteenth day of the month, at even, in the plains of Jericho.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they ate of the old corn of the land on the morrow after the passover, unleavened loaves, and roasted [corn] on that same day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the manna ceased on the morrow, when they had eaten of the old corn of the land; and there was no more manna for the children of Israel; and they ate of the produce of the land of Canaan that year.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Joshua was by Jericho, that he lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, there stood a man before him with his sword drawn in his hand. And Joshua went to him, and said to him: Art thou for us, or for our enemies?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, No; for [as] captain of the army of Jehovah am I now come. Then Joshua fell upon his face to the earth, and worshipped, and said to him, What saith my lord unto his servant?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the captain of Jehovah's army said to Joshua, Loose thy sandal from off thy foot: for the place whereon thou standest is holy. And Joshua did so.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Jericho was shut up and was barred, because of the children of Israel: none went out, and none came in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Joshua, See, I have given into thy hand Jericho, and the king thereof, [and] the valiant men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall go round the city, all the men of war, encompassing the city once. Thus shalt thou do six days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And seven priests shall carry before the ark seven blast-trumpets; and on the seventh day ye shall go round the city seven times, and the priests shall blow with the trumpets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass when they make a long blast with the blast-horn, that all the people on hearing the sound of the trumpet shall shout with a great shout; and the wall of the city shall fall flat, and the people shall go up, each one straight before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua the son of Nun called the priests, and said to them, Carry the ark of the covenant, and seven priests shall carry seven blast-trumpets before the ark of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to the people, Pass on, go round the city, and they that are armed shall pass on before the ark of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Joshua had spoken to the people, that the seven priests carrying the seven blast-trumpets before Jehovah passed on and blew with the trumpets; and the ark of the covenant of Jehovah went after them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the armed men went before the priests who blew with the trumpets, and the rearguard came after the ark; they blew with the trumpets in marching.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua had commanded the people, saying, Ye shall not shout, nor let your voice be heard, neither shall a word proceed out of your mouth, until the day I say to you, Shout; then shall ye shout.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the ark of Jehovah went round the city, encompassing [it] once; and they came into the camp, and lodged in the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua rose early in the morning, and the priests carried the ark of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the seven priests carrying the seven blast-trumpets before the ark of Jehovah went on and blew continually with the trumpets; and the armed men went before them, and the rearguard went after the ark of Jehovah; they blew with the trumpets in marching.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the second day they went round the city once, and returned into the camp. So they did six days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was so that on the seventh day they rose early, about the morning-dawn, and went round the city after the same manner seven times; only on that day they went round the city seven times.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass the seventh time, when the priests blew with the trumpets, that Joshua said to the people, Shout; for Jehovah has given you the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the city shall be accursed, it and all that is in it, to Jehovah; only Rahab the harlot shall live, she and all that are with her in the house, because she hid the messengers that we sent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But in any wise keep from the accursed thing, lest ye make [yourselves] accursed in taking of the accursed thing, and make the camp of Israel a curse, and trouble it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the silver, and gold, and vessels of copper and iron, shall be holy to Jehovah; they shall come into the treasury of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people shouted, and they blew with the trumpets. And it came to pass when the people heard the sound of the trumpets, and the people shouted with a great shout, that the wall fell down flat; and the people went up into the city, each one straight before him, and they took the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they utterly destroyed all that was in the city; both man and woman, young and old, and ox, and sheep, and ass, with the edge of the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua said to the two men that had spied out the country, Go into the harlot's house and bring out thence the woman, and all that she has, as ye swore unto her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the young men, the spies, went in and brought out Rahab, and her father, and her mother, and her brethren, and all that she had: all her kindred did they bring out, and they left them outside the camp of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they burned the city with fire, and all that was therein; only the silver, and the gold, and the vessels of copper and of iron, they put into the treasury of the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua saved alive Rahab the harlot, and her father's household, and all that she had, and she dwelt in the midst of Israel to this day; because she hid the messengers whom Joshua had sent to spy out Jericho.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua swore at that time, saying, Cursed be the man before Jehovah who shall rise up and build this city Jericho! In his first-born shall he lay its foundation, and in his youngest son shall he set up its gates.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah was with Joshua; and his fame was in all the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the children of Israel committed unfaithfulness in that which had been brought under the curse: Achan, the son of Carmi, the son of Zabdi, the son of Zerah, of the tribe of Judah, took of the accursed thing; and the anger of Jehovah was kindled against the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua sent men from Jericho to Ai, which is beside Beth-Aven, on the east side of Bethel, and spoke to them, saying, Go up and spy out the country. And the men went up and spied out Ai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they returned to Joshua, and said to him, Let not all the people go up; let about two or three thousand men go up and smite Ai; make not all the people to toil thither, for they are few.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there went up thither of the people about three thousand men, but they fled before the men of Ai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Ai smote of them about thirty-six men; and they pursued them from before the gate to Shebarim, and smote them on the descent. Then the hearts of the people melted, and became as water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua rent his clothes, and fell to the earth upon his face before the ark of Jehovah until the evening, he and the elders of Israel, and threw dust upon their heads.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua said, Alas, Lord Jehovah, wherefore hast thou at all brought this people over the Jordan, to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites, to destroy us? Oh that we had been content and had remained beyond the Jordan!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ah Lord! what shall I say after Israel have turned their backs before their enemies?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the Canaanites and all the inhabitants of the land shall hear [of it], they will surround us, and cut off our name from the earth. And what wilt thou do unto thy great name?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Joshua, Rise up; wherefore liest thou thus upon thy face?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Israel hath sinned, and they have also transgressed my covenant which I commanded them, and they have even taken of the accursed thing, and have also stolen, and dissembled also, and they have put it among their stuff.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel shall not be able to stand before their enemies: they shall turn their backs before their enemies, for they have made themselves accursed. I will no more be with you, except ye destroy the accursed thing from your midst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rise up, hallow the people, and say, Hallow yourselves for to-morrow; for thus saith Jehovah theGod of Israel, There is an accursed thing in the midst of thee, Israel: thou shalt not be able to stand before thine enemies, until ye take away the accursed thing from your midst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall be brought near in the morning according to your tribes; and it shall be, that the tribe which Jehovah taketh shall come forward by families, and the family which Jehovah taketh shall come forward by households; and the household which Jehovah taketh shall come forward man by man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be, that he who is taken with the accursed thing shall be burned with fire, he and all that he hath, because he hath transgressed the covenant of Jehovah, and because he hath wrought wickedness in Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua rose early in the morning, and caused Israel to come forward by their tribes, and the tribe of Judah was taken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he caused the families of Judah to come forward, and he took the family of the Zarhites. And he caused the family of the Zarhites to come forward man by man, and Zabdi was taken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he caused his household to come forward man by man, and Achan, the son of Carmi, the son of Zabdi, the son of Zerah, of the tribe of Judah, was taken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua said to Achan, My son, give, I pray thee, glory to Jehovah theGod of Israel, and make confession to him: tell me now what thou hast done, keep it not back from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Achan answered Joshua and said, Indeed I have sinned against Jehovah theGod of Israel, and thus and thus have I done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I saw among the spoils a beautiful mantle of Shinar, and two hundred shekels of silver, and a golden bar of fifty shekels weight, and I coveted them and took them; and behold, they are hid in the earth in the midst of my tent, and the silver under it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua sent messengers, and they ran to the tent, and behold, it was hid in his tent, and the silver under it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took them out of the midst of the tent, and brought them to Joshua and to all the children of Israel, and laid them out before Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Joshua, and all Israel with him, took Achan the son of Zerah, and the silver, and the mantle, and the bar of gold, and his sons, and his daughters, and his oxen, and his asses, and his sheep, and his tent, and all that he had; and they brought them up into the valley of Achor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua said, How hast thou troubled us! Jehovah will trouble thee this day. And all Israel stoned him with stones; and they burned them with fire, and stoned them with stones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they raised over him a great heap of stones, [which is there] to this day. And Jehovah turned from the fierceness of his anger. Therefore the name of that place was called, The Valley of Achor, to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Joshua, Fear not, neither be dismayed. Take with thee all the people of war, and arise, go up to Ai. See, I have given into thy hand the king of Ai, and his people, and his city, and his land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt do to Ai and to its king as thou didst to Jericho and to its king; only, the spoil thereof and the cattle thereof shall ye take as prey for yourselves. Set an ambush against the city behind it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua arose, and all the people of war, to go up to Ai. And Joshua chose thirty thousand valiant men, and sent them away by night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he commanded them, saying, See, ye shall be in ambush against the city, behind the city: go not very far from the city, and be all of you ready.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I and all the people that are with me will approach to the city; and it shall come to pass when they come out against us, as at the first, that we will flee before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they will come out after us till we have drawn them from the city; for they will say, They flee before us, as at the first; and we will flee before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall rise up from the ambush and take possession of the city; and Jehovah yourGod will deliver it into your hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be when ye have taken the city, that ye shall set the city on fire; according to the word of Jehovah shall ye do. See, I have commanded you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua sent them forth; and they went to lie in ambush, and abode between Bethel and Ai, on the west of Ai. And Joshua lodged that night among the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua rose early in the morning, and inspected the people, and went up, he and the elders of Israel, before the people to Ai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people of war that were with him went up, and drew near, and came before the city; and they encamped on the north of Ai; and the valley was between them and Ai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now he had taken about five thousand men, and set them in ambush between Bethel and Ai, on the west of the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they had set the people, the whole camp on the north of the city, and their ambush on the west of the city, Joshua went that night into the midst of the valley.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the king of Ai saw it, that the men of the city hasted and rose early, and went out against Israel to battle, he and all his people, at the appointed place before the plain. But he knew not that there was an ambush against him behind the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua and all Israel let themselves be beaten before them; and they fled by the way of the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people that were in the city were called together to pursue after them; and they pursued after Joshua, and were drawn away from the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And not a man remained in Ai and Bethel that went not out after Israel; and they left the city open, and pursued after Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Joshua, Stretch out the javelin that is in thy hand toward Ai; for I will give it into thy hand. And Joshua stretched out the javelin that he had in his hand toward the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the ambush arose quickly from their place, and they ran when he stretched out his hand, and came into the city, and took it, and hasted and set the city on fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Ai turned and saw, and behold, the smoke of the city went up to heaven, and they had no power to flee this way or that way; and the people that fled to the wilderness turned upon the pursuers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When Joshua and all Israel saw that the ambush had taken the city, and that the smoke of the city went up, they turned again, and slew the men of Ai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the others went out of the city against them; so they were in the midst of Israel, some on this side, and some on that side; and they smote them, until they let none of them escape or flee away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Ai they took alive, and brought him to Joshua.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Israel had ended slaying all the inhabitants of Ai in the field, in the wilderness wherein they had chased them, and they had all fallen by the edge of the sword, until they were consumed, that all Israel returned to Ai, and smote it with the edge of the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And so it was, that all who fell that day, men as well as women, were twelve thousand, all the people of Ai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua did not draw back his hand, which he had stretched out with the javelin, until they had utterly destroyed all the inhabitants of Ai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only, the cattle and the spoil of the city Israel took as prey to themselves, according to the word of Jehovah which he had commanded Joshua.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua burned Ai, and made it an everlasting heap of desolation to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Ai he hanged on a tree until the evening; and at the going down of the sun Joshua commanded, and they took his carcase down from the tree, and threw it down at the entrance of the gate of the city, and raised upon it a great heap of stones, [which remains] to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Joshua built an altar to Jehovah theGod of Israel, in mount Ebal,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as Moses the servant of Jehovah had commanded the children of Israel, as it is written in the book of the law of Moses, an altar of whole stones, over which iron had not been lifted up. And they offered up burnt-offerings on it to Jehovah, and sacrificed peace-offerings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he wrote there on the stones a copy of the law of Moses, which he had written before the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all Israel, and their elders, and their officers and judges, stood on this side and on that side of the ark before the priests the Levites, who bore the ark of the covenant of Jehovah, as well the stranger as the home-born [Israelite]; half of them toward mount Gerizim, and the other half of them toward mount Ebal; as Moses the servant of Jehovah had commanded, that they should bless the people of Israel, in the beginning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And afterwards he read all the words of the law, the blessing and the curse, according to all that is written in the book of the law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There was not a word of all that Moses had commanded which Joshua read not before the whole congregation of Israel, and the women, and the children, and the strangers that lived among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when all the kings who were on this side the Jordan, in the hill-country, and in the lowland, and along all the coast of the great sea as far as opposite to Lebanon, the Hittite, and the Amorite, the Canaanite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite, heard [of it],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that they assembled together, to fight with Joshua and with Israel, with one accord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the inhabitants of Gibeon heard what Joshua had done to Jericho and to Ai,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then they also acted with craft, and they went prepared as on a journey, and took old sacks upon their asses, and wine-flasks, old and rent and tied up;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and old and patched sandals upon their feet, and old garments upon them; and all the bread of their provision was dry [and] mouldy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went to Joshua unto the camp at Gilgal, and said to him, and to the men of Israel, From a far country are we come; and now make a covenant with us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Israel said to the Hivite, Perhaps thou dwellest in the midst of us, and how should I make a covenant with thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said unto Joshua, We are thy servants. And Joshua said to them, Who are ye? and from whence come ye?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, From a very far country are thy servants come, because of the name of Jehovah thyGod; for we have heard the fame of him, and all that he did in Egypt,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all that he did to the two kings of the Amorites that were beyond Jordan, to Sihon the king of Heshbon, and to Og the king of Bashan, who was at Ashtaroth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And our elders and all the inhabitants of our country spoke to us, saying, Take victuals in your hand for the way, and go to meet them, and say to them, We are your servants, and now make a covenant with us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This our bread we took warm for our provision out of our houses on the day we came forth to go unto you; and now, behold, it is dry, and is become mouldy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these flasks of wine which we filled new, behold, they are rent; and these our garments and our sandals are become old by reason of the very long journey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men took of their victuals, but they did not inquire at the mouth of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua made peace with them, and made a covenant with them, to let them live; and the princes of the assembly swore unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the end of three days after they had made a covenant with them, that they heard that they were their neighbours, and that they dwelt in their midst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel journeyed, and came to their cities on the third day; and their cities were Gibeon, and Chephirah, and Beeroth, and Kirjath-jearim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel did not smite them, because the princes of the assembly had sworn unto them by Jehovah theGod of Israel. Then all the assembly murmured against the princes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the princes said to all the assembly, We have sworn unto them by Jehovah theGod of Israel, and now we may not touch them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This we will do to them, and let them live, lest wrath come upon us, because of the oath which we swore unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the princes said to them, Let them live. And they were hewers of wood and drawers of water for all the assembly; as the princes had said to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua called for them, and he spoke to them, saying, Why have ye deceived us, saying, We are very far from you; whereas ye dwell in our midst?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now ye are cursed, and ye shall never cease to be bondmen, and hewers of wood, and drawers of water for the house of myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they answered Joshua and said, Because it was certainly told thy servants how that Jehovah thyGod commanded his servant Moses to give you all the land, and to destroy all the inhabitants of the land from before you; and we feared greatly for our lives because of you, and did this thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now behold, we are in thy hand: as it is good and right in thine eyes to do to us, do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he did so to them, and delivered them out of the hand of the children of Israel, and they did not slay them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua made them that day hewers of wood and drawers of water for the assembly, and for the altar of Jehovah, to this day, in the place which he should choose.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem heard that Joshua had taken Ai and had utterly destroyed it, that he had done to Ai and its king as he had done to Jericho and its king, and that the inhabitants of Gibeon had made peace with Israel, and were among them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that they feared greatly; for Gibeon was a great city, as one of the royal cities, and it was greater than Ai, and all its men were mighty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem sent to Hoham king of Hebron, and to Piream king of Jarmuth, and to Japhia king of Lachish, and to Debir king of Eglon, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come up to me, and help me, that we may smite Gibeon; for it has made peace with Joshua and with the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the five kings of the Amorites, the king of Jerusalem, the king of Hebron, the king of Jarmuth, the king of Lachish, [and] the king of Eglon, gathered themselves together, and went up, they and all their armies, and they encamped before Gibeon, and made war against it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Gibeon sent to Joshua, to the camp at Gilgal, saying, Withdraw not thy hand from thy servants: come up to us quickly, and save us and help us; for all the kings of the Amorites that dwell in the hill-country are gathered against us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So Joshua went up from Gilgal, he and all the people of war with him, even all the valiant men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Joshua, Fear them not; for into thy hand have I given them: not a man of them shall stand before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua came upon them suddenly; he went up from Gilgal all night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah discomfited them before Israel, and smote them [with] a great slaughter at Gibeon; and he chased them on the way of the ascent of Beth-horon, and smote them up to Azekah and Makkedah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, as they fled from before Israel, — they were at the descent of Beth-horon, — that Jehovah cast down great stones from heaven upon them up to Azekah, and they died. They were more who died with the hailstones than they whom the children of Israel had slain with the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then spoke Joshua to Jehovah in the day when Jehovah gave up the Amorites before the children of Israel, and he said in the sight of Israel, Sun, stand still upon Gibeon; And thou, moon, in the valley of Ajalon!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sun stood still, and the moon remained where it was, until the nation had avenged themselves upon their enemies. Is not this written in the book of Jasher? And the sun remained standing in the midst of heaven, and hasted not to go down about a full day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was no day like that before it or after it, that Jehovah hearkened to the voice of a man; for Jehovah fought for Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, to the camp, to Gilgal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these five kings fled, and hid themselves in the cave at Makkedah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told Joshua, saying, The five kings have been found, hid in the cave at Makkedah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua said, Roll great stones before the mouth of the cave, and set men before it to keep them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye, stay not, pursue after your enemies, and smite the hindmost of them; suffer them not to enter into their cities; for Jehovah yourGod has given them into your hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Joshua and the children of Israel had ended smiting them with a very great slaughter, until they were consumed, that the remnant which remained of them entered into fortified cities;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the people returned to the camp to Joshua, at Makkedah, in peace; none moved his tongue against the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua said, Open the mouth of the cave, and bring forth to me those five kings out of the cave.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they did so, and brought forth to him those five kings out of the cave, the king of Jerusalem, the king of Hebron, the king of Jarmuth, the king of Lachish, the king of Eglon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when they had brought forth those kings to Joshua, that Joshua called to all the men of Israel, and said to the captains of the men of war who went with him, Come forward, put your feet on the necks of these kings. And they came forward and put their feet on their necks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua said to them, Fear not, neither be dismayed; be strong and courageous, for thus will Jehovah do to all your enemies against whom ye fight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And afterwards Joshua smote them, and put them to death, and hanged them on five trees; and they were hanging upon the trees until the evening.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the time of the going down of the sun, that Joshua commanded, and they took them down off the trees, and cast them into the cave where they had been hid, and laid great stones before the cave's mouth, [which remain] to this very day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua took Makkedah on that day, and smote it with the edge of the sword, and the king thereof, him and all the souls that were therein he utterly destroyed; he let none remain; and he did to the king of Makkedah as he had done to the king of Jericho.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua passed, and all Israel with him, from Makkedah to Libnah, and fought against Libnah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah gave it also and the king thereof into the hand of Israel; and he smote it with the edge of the sword, and all the souls that were therein; he let none remain in it: and he did to the king thereof as he had done to the king of Jericho.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua passed, and all Israel with him, from Libnah to Lachish, and encamped against it, and fought against it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah gave Lachish into the hand of Israel; and they took it on the second day, and smote it with the edge of the sword, and all the souls that were therein, according to all that he had done to Libnah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Horam king of Gezer came up to help Lachish; and Joshua smote him and his people, until he had left him none remaining.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua, and all Israel with him, passed from Lachish to Eglon; and they encamped against it, and fought against it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took it on that day, and smote it with the edge of the sword; and all the souls that were therein he utterly destroyed on that day, according to all that he had done to Lachish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua went up, and all Israel with him, from Eglon to Hebron; and they fought against it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took it, and smote it with the edge of the sword, and the king thereof, and all the cities thereof, and all the souls that were therein: he let none remain, according to all that he had done to Eglon; and he utterly destroyed it, and all the souls that were therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, to Debir; and fought against it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took it, and the king thereof, and all the cities thereof, and they smote them with the edge of the sword, and utterly destroyed all the souls that were therein; he let none remain: as he had done to Hebron, and as he had done to Libnah, and to the king thereof, so he did to Debir and to the king thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua smote the whole country, the mountain, and the south, and the lowland, and the hill-slopes, and all their kings: he let none remain, but he utterly destroyed all that breathed, as Jehovah theGod of Israel had commanded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua smote them from Kadesh-barnea even to Gazah, and all the country of Goshen, even to Gibeon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all these kings and their land did Joshua take at one time; for Jehovah theGod of Israel fought for Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, to the camp, to Gilgal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Jabin king of Hazor heard [this], that he sent to Jobab king of Madon, and to the king of Shimron, and to the king of Achshaph,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to the kings that were northward in the mountains, and in the plain south of Chinneroth, and in the lowland, and on the upland of Dor on the west,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to the Canaanite on the east and on the west, and to the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Jebusite in the mountains, and to the Hivite at the foot of Hermon in the land of Mizpah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went out, they and all their armies with them, a people numerous as the sand that is on the seashore in multitude, with horses and chariots very many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all these kings met together, and came and encamped together at the waters of Merom, to fight against Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Joshua, Be not afraid because of them; for to-morrow about this time will I give them all up slain before Israel: their horses shalt thou hough, and thou shalt burn their chariots with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua, and all the people of war with him, came upon them by the waters of Merom suddenly, and fell upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah delivered them into the hand of Israel, and they smote them, and chased them unto great Zidon, and to Misrephoth-maim, and to the valley of Mizpah eastward, and smote them until none were left remaining to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua did to them as Jehovah had said to him: he houghed their horses, and burned their chariots with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua turned back at that time, and took Hazor, and smote the king thereof with the sword; for Hazor was in times past the head of all those kingdoms.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they smote all the souls that were therein with the edge of the sword, destroying them utterly: there was not any left to breathe; and he burned Hazor with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the cities of those kings and all their kings did Joshua take; and he smote them with the edge of the sword, destroying them utterly, as Moses the servant of Jehovah had commanded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only, all the cities that stood still upon their hills Israel did not burn, save Hazor alone, [which] Joshua burned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the spoil of these cities and the cattle the children of Israel took as prey to themselves; only, they smote all the men with the edge of the sword, until they had destroyed them: they left none that breathed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As Jehovah had commanded Moses his servant, so did Moses command Joshua, and so did Joshua: he let nothing fail of all that Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua took all that land, the mountain and all the south, and all the land of Goshen, and the lowland, and the plain, and the mountain of Israel, and its lowland;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: from the smooth mountain, which rises toward Seir, as far as Baal-Gad in the valley of Lebanon, at the foot of mount Hermon; and he took all their kings, and smote them, and put them to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Joshua made war a long time with all those kings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There was not a city that made peace with the children of Israel, save the Hivites who dwelt at Gibeon; they took all in battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it was of Jehovah that their heart was hardened, to meet Israel in battle, that they might be utterly destroyed, and that there might be no favour shewn to them, but that they might be destroyed, as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua came at that time and cut off the Anakim from the mountains, from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the mountain of Judah, and from all the mountain of Israel: Joshua destroyed them utterly, with their cities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There were none of the Anakim left in the land of the children of Israel; only at Gazah, at Gath, and at Ashdod there remained.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua took the whole land, according to all that Jehovah had said to Moses; and Joshua gave it for an inheritance to Israel according to their divisions, by their tribes. And the land rested from war.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the kings of the land, whom the children of Israel smote, and of whose land they took possession across the Jordan, toward the sun-rising, from the river Arnon to mount Hermon, and all the plain on the east:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sihon the king of the Amorites, who dwelt at Heshbon, [and] ruled from Aroer, which is upon the bank of the river Arnon, and from the middle of the ravine, and over half Gilead, as far as the river Jabbok, [which is] the border of the children of Ammon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the plain as far as the sea of Chinneroth on the east, and as far as the sea of the plain, the salt sea, on the east, toward Beth-jeshimoth; and on the south, under the slopes of Pisgah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the territory of Og the king of Bashan, of the residue of the giants, who dwelt at Ashtaroth and at Edrei,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ruled over mount Hermon, and over Salcah, and over all Bashan, as far as the border of the Geshurites and the Maachathites, and [over] half Gilead [as far as] the border of Sihon the king of Heshbon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moses the servant of Jehovah and the children of Israel smote them, and Moses the servant of Jehovah gave it for a possession to the Reubenites, and to the Gadites, and to half the tribe of Manasseh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the kings of the land whom Joshua and the children of Israel smote on this side the Jordan on the west, from Baal-Gad in the valley of Lebanon as far as the smooth mountain, which rises toward Seir. And Joshua gave it to the tribes of Israel for a possession according to their divisions,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in the mountain, and in the lowland, and in the plain, and on the hill-slopes, and in the wilderness, and in the south: the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Canaanites, the Perizzites, the Hivites and the Jebusites:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The king of Jericho, one; the king of Ai, which is beside Bethel, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Jerusalem, one; the king of Hebron, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Jarmuth, one; the king of Lachish, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Eglon, one; the king of Gezer, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Debir, one; the king of Geder, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Hormah, one; the king of Arad, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Libnah, one; the king of Adullam, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Makkedah, one; the king of Bethel, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Tappuah, one; the king of Hepher, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Aphek, one; the king of Lasharon, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Madon, one; the king of Hazor, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Shimron-meron, one; the king of Achshaph, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Taanach, one; the king of Megiddo, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Kedesh, one; the king of Jokneam on Carmel, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Dor in the upland of Dor, one; the king of Goim, at Gilgal, one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the king of Tirzah, one: all the kings thirty and one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua was old, advanced in age, and Jehovah said to him, Thou art old, advanced in age, and there remaineth yet very much land to take possession of.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the land that yet remaineth: all the districts of the Philistines and all the Geshurites,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: from the Shihor, which [floweth] before Egypt, as far as the borders of Ekron northward, [and which] is counted to the Canaanite; five lordships of the Philistines: of Gazah, and of Ashdod, of Eshkalon, of Gath, and of Ekron; also the Avvites;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in the south, the whole land of the Canaanites, and Mearah which [belongeth] to the Sidonians, unto Aphek, to the border of the Amorites;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the land of the Giblites, and all Lebanon, toward the sun-rising, from Baal-Gad at the foot of mount Hermon to the entrance into Hamath;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: all the inhabitants of the hill-country from Lebanon to Misrephoth-maim, all the Sidonians; I will dispossess them from before the children of Israel. Only, partition it by lot to Israel for an inheritance, as I have commanded thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now divide this land for an inheritance to the nine tribes, and to half the tribe of Manasseh;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: with him the Reubenites and the Gadites have received their inheritance, which Moses gave them, beyond the Jordan eastward, as Moses the servant of Jehovah gave them:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: from Aroer, which is on the bank of the river Arnon, and the city that is in the midst of the ravine, and all the plateau of Medeba to Dibon,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the cities of Sihon the king of the Amorites, who reigned at Heshbon, to the border of the children of Ammon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Gilead, and the border of the Geshurites and Maachathites, and all mount Hermon, and the whole of Bashan to Salcah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: all the kingdom of Og in Bashan, who reigned at Ashtaroth and at Edrei, who remained of the residue of the giants; and Moses smote them and dispossessed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the children of Israel did not dispossess the Geshurites nor the Maachathites; but the Geshurites and the Maachathites dwell among the Israelites to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only to the tribe of Levi he gave no inheritance: the offerings by fire of Jehovah theGod of Israel are their inheritance, as he said to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses gave [a portion] to the tribe of the children of Reuben according to their families.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their territory was from Aroer, which is on the bank of the river Arnon, and the city that is in the midst of the ravine, and all the plateau by Medeba;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Heshbon, and all her cities that are in the plateau, Dibon, and Bamoth-Baal, and Beth-Baal-meon,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jahzah, and Kedemoth, and Mephaath,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Kirjathaim, and Sibmah, and Zereth-shahar in the mountain of the vale,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Beth-Peor, and the slopes of Pisgah, and Beth-jeshimoth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: all the cities of the plateau, and the whole kingdom of Sihon the king of the Amorites, who reigned at Heshbon, whom Moses smote, him and the princes of Midian, Evi, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba, the chiefs of Sihon dwelling in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Balaam the son of Beor, the diviner, did the children of Israel kill with the sword among them that were slain by them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the border of the children of Reuben was the Jordan, and [its] border. This is the inheritance of the children of Reuben according to their families, the cities and their hamlets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses gave [a portion] to the tribe of Gad, to the children of Gad according to their families.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their territory was Jaazer, and all the cities of Gilead, and half the land of the children of Ammon, to Aroer which is before Rabbah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and from Heshbon to Ramath-Mizpeh, and Betonim; and from Mahanaim to the border of Debir;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in the valley, Beth-haram, and Beth-Nimrah, and Succoth, and Zaphon, the rest of the kingdom of Sihon the king of Heshbon, the Jordan and [its] border, as far as the edge of the sea of Chinnereth beyond the Jordan eastward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the inheritance of the children of Gad according to their families, the cities and their hamlets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses gave [a portion] to half the tribe of Manasseh; and for half the tribe of the children of Manasseh according to their families:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: their territory was from Mahanaim, all Bashan, the whole kingdom of Og the king of Bashan, and all the villages of Jair, which are in Bashan, sixty cities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And half Gilead, and Ashtaroth, and Edrei, the cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan, [belonged] to the children of Machir the son of Manasseh, to the one half of the children of Machir according to their families.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is that which Moses allotted for inheritance in the plains of Moab, beyond the Jordan of Jericho, eastward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But to the tribe of Levi Moses gave no inheritance: Jehovah theGod of Israel is their inheritance, as he said to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is what the children of Israel inherited in the land of Canaan, which Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun, and the chief fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel, allotted for inheritance to them:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: their inheritance was by lot, as Jehovah had commanded by Moses, for the nine tribes, and the half tribe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Moses had given the inheritance of the two tribes and the half tribe beyond the Jordan, but to the Levites he had given no inheritance among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the children of Joseph were two tribes, Manasseh and Ephraim; and they gave no part to the Levites in the land, save cities to dwell in, and their suburbs for their cattle and for their substance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As Jehovah had commanded Moses, so the children of Israel did, and they divided the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the children of Judah came near to Joshua in Gilgal, and Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite said to him, Thou knowest the word that Jehovah spoke to Moses the man ofGod, concerning me and thee, in Kadesh-barnea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Forty years old was I when Moses the servant of Jehovah sent me from Kadesh-barnea to search out the land; and I brought him word again as it was in my heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And my brethren that had gone up with me made the heart of the people melt; but I wholly followed Jehovah myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses swore on that day, saying, The land whereon thy feet have trodden shall assuredly be thine inheritance, and thy children's for ever! for thou hast wholly followed Jehovah myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now behold, Jehovah has kept me alive, as he said, these forty-five years, since Jehovah spoke this word to Moses, when Israel wandered in the wilderness; and now behold, I am this day eighty-five years old.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am still this day strong, as in the day that Moses sent me: as my strength was then, even so is my strength now, for war, both to go out and to come in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now give me this mountain, of which Jehovah spoke in that day; for thou heardest in that day that Anakim are there, and great fortified cities. If so be Jehovah shall be with me, then I shall dispossess them, as Jehovah said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua blessed him, and gave Hebron to Caleb the son of Jephunneh for an inheritance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hebron therefore became the inheritance of Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite to this day, because he wholly followed Jehovah theGod of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the name of Hebron before was Kirjath-Arba; the great man among the Anakim. And the land rested from war.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the lot of the tribe of the children of Judah according to their families was: to the border of Edom, the wilderness of Zin, southward, in the extreme south.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their southern border was from the end of the salt sea, from the tongue that turns southward;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and it went out south of the ascent of Akrabbim, and passed on to Zin, and went up on the south of Kadesh-barnea, and passed to Hezron, and went up toward Addar, and turned toward Karkaah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and passed on to Azmon, and went out by the torrent of Egypt; and the border ended at the sea. That shall be your border southward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the eastern border was the salt sea as far as the end of the Jordan. — And the border on the north side was from the tongue of the salt sea, at the end of the Jordan;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the border went up toward Beth-hoglah, and passed north of Beth-Arabah; and the border went up to the stone of Bohan, the son of Reuben;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the border went up toward Debir from the valley of Achor, and turned northward to Gilgal, which is opposite to the ascent of Adummim, which is on the south side of the torrent; and the border passed to the waters of En-shemesh, and ended at En-rogel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the border went up to the valley of the son of Hinnom, toward the south side of the Jebusite, that is, Jerusalem; and the border went up to the top of the mountain that is before the valley of Hinnom westward, which is at the end of the valley of Rephaim northward;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the border reached along from the top of the mountain toward the spring of the waters of Nephtoah, and went out toward the cities of mount Ephron; and the border reached along to Baalah, that is, Kirjath-jearim;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the border turned from Baalah westwards toward mount Seir, and passed toward the side of Har-jearim, that is, Chesalon, northwards, and went down to Beth-shemesh, and passed Timnah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the border went out to the side of Ekron northwards, and the border reached along toward Shicron, and passed mount Baalah, and went toward Jabneel; and the border ended at the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the west border is the great sea and [its] coast. This is the border of the children of Judah round about, according to their families.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And to Caleb the son of Jephunneh he gave a portion among the children of Judah according to the commandment of Jehovah to Joshua, the city of Arba, the father of Anak, that is, Hebron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thence Caleb dispossessed the three sons of Anak, Sheshai and Ahiman and Talmai, who were born of Anak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And from there he went up against the inhabitants of Debir; now the name of Debir before was Kirjath-sepher.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Caleb said, He that smites Kirjath-sepher and takes it, to him will I give Achsah my daughter as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Othniel the son of Kenaz, the brother of Caleb, took it; and he gave him Achsah his daughter as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as she came, that she urged him to ask of her father a field; and she sprang down from the ass. And Caleb said to her, What wouldest thou?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said, Give me a blessing; for thou hast given me a southern land; give me also springs of water. Then he gave her the upper springs and the lower springs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Judah according to their families.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The cities at the extremity of the tribe of the children of Judah, toward the border of Edom in the south, were: Kabzeel, and Eder, and Jagur,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Kinah, and Dimonah, and Adadah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Kedesh, and Hazor, and Jithnan,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ziph, and Telem, and Bealoth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Hazor-hadattah, and Kerioth-Hezron, that is Hazor,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Amam, and Shema, and Molada,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Hazar-gaddah, and Heshmon, and Beth-Pelet,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Hazar-Shual, and Beer-sheba, and Biziothiah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Baalah, and Ijim, and Ezem,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Eltolad, and Chesil, and Hormah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Ziklag, and Madmannah, and Sansanna,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Lebaoth, and Shilhim, and Ain, and Rimmon: all the cities twenty-nine, and their hamlets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — In the lowland: Eshtaol, and Zoreah, and Ashnah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Zanoah, and En-gannim, Tappuah and Enam,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jarmuth and Adullam, Sochoh and Azekah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
